author: 0xrubo
content: after digesting this a bit more, i think having execution requests as an opaque byte array for zkvm guests is a better choice. here's why. previously, we were taking  the byte array and computing the hash. now, we have to decode ssz, construct an object, then encode that object to a byte array to compute hash. thus, it's nothing but a burden on the guest app in an already very limited environment. it would make more sense if those structures were usable in the guest. do i miss something?

author: jsign
content:
Yes, for the EL is easier to receive the bytes and operate with them.. but the tension as I mentioned erlier is with proof verifier that is the CL.  The CL have to verify the `hash_tree_root(...)` of `NewPayloadRequest`. Today we stick with `NewPayloadRequest` definition of `consensus-specs` since we want the "API" for CLs (which are main proof users) is as smooth as possible.

If we make this change to help the guest program side, now the CL have to create a separate definition of `NewPayloadRequest` which is different from `consensus-specs` -- while that a priori can simply be to encode the requests in those opaque bytes, I think this has other implications since in theory zkCLs must not require the full `NewPayloadRequest` to check public input (i.e. that means always having to download the full block, which increases "linearly" with gas limit increases).  The idea is that they can only work with `NewPayloadRequestHeader` since `hash_tree_root(NewPayloadRequestHeader) == hash_tree_root(NewPaylaodRequest)` . If we change `NewPayloadRequest` definition from `consensus-specs` to something else, we must be sure we aren't complicating how `NewPayloadRequestHeader` is provided at the CL layer. 

If this isn't a big deal for CL, I personally don't have any prob with doing the change.

prob <@402080271563751435> can have a more detailed opinion on this CL point (i.e. `NewPaylaodRequestHeader` consequences)
(cc <@531934490826768418>  too since I think he can also help us with this!)

author: taulepton_
content:
Added a task to review the implications from the perspective of “transaction-less” payload validation. Will provide feedback when I’ve reviewed. 

That makes me think actually is there a canonical term for payload validation in absence of the full payload body 🤔

author: taulepton_
content: Out of interest what is the typical size for this data?

author: taulepton_
content:
It would be good to understand how much additional prover work this additional encoding step introduces. I think this boils down to a balance of simplicity in CL / EL + efficiency trade off. 

My first impression is that keep as is with ssz will make things simpler on aggregate. Providing it’s not a big source of inefficiency in the guest. Furthermore if we intend on standardising the engine API to use SSZ then it should disappear in the future?
